title: PCR Amplification Step-Through Simulator
description: Step through one cycle of the polymerase chain reaction — Denaturation (94°C), Annealing (60°C), and Extension (72°C) — and watch the DNA transform at each stage. A temperature gauge, step description, cycle counter, and an exponential copy counter track the reaction; 'Run Cycle' and 'Run 5 Cycles' show the doubling, and clicking any DNA part (template strand, primer, new strand, polymerase) reveals its definition.
creator: Forensic Science Intelligent Textbook
subject: Forensic Science
DNA Profiling
Polymerase Chain Reaction
Molecular Biology
DNA Amplification
bloomLevel: Understand (L2) — explain the three steps of PCR by tracing what happens to the DNA at each stage.

educational.topic: The three steps of the polymerase chain reaction (PCR)
learningObjectives: Explain what happens to the DNA during denaturation, annealing, and extension, and the temperature each step requires.
Describe the roles of the template strand, primers, and Taq polymerase in copying DNA.
Explain why each completed cycle doubles the number of target copies, producing exponential amplification.
educational.prerequisites: Basic structure of DNA (double helix, complementary base pairing); idea that DNA can be copied

pedagogical.interactionPattern: The student presses 'Next Step' to advance through Denaturation → Annealing → Extension one stage at a time, or 'Run Cycle' / 'Run 5 Cycles' to auto-play with pauses. A molecular view animates the two template strands (red and blue), base-pair bridges breaking during denaturation, yellow primers binding during annealing, and a light-purple new strand growing from each primer with a polymerase enzyme during extension. A side panel shows a temperature gauge, the current step name and description, a cycle counter, and a copy counter that doubles each cycle. Clicking any DNA part shows a definition pop-up.
pedagogical.assessment: Formative, prediction-friendly: the step-by-step control lets the student predict what the strands will do before each stage is revealed, and the temperature and copy-count readouts give concrete data to explain why each step occurs and how amplification grows exponentially.
pedagogical.modelType: Staged animation model: a step index (0-2) selects the active stage; a per-step progress value drives the visual transformation (strand separation, bridge fade, primer binding, new-strand growth) and the temperature interpolates toward the step's target; completing extension increments the cycle and doubles the copy count (copies = 2^cycle).
controls: Run Cycle button (auto-run all three stages once)
Clickable DNA parts (template strand, primer, new strand, polymerase) for definitions
